document.title: Usability Validation Plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation test plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector, Report No. A-TCF-5-039 Rev.0, dated APR/18/2020.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines the plan for conducting usability testing during the development of ADD, including participants, methods, metrics, tasks, goals, reporting, and references.
keyPoints: Usability testing goals include establishing baseline user performance, validating performance measures, and identifying design concerns.
A total of 7 participants should meet the stated user profile for the usability test.
Scenario completion success rates, dialog script adherence, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion will be collected.
Completion rate goal is 100% for each usability task.
Total errors below 4 is the goal for each usability task.
Referenced standards include EN ISO 14971:2007, EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015, and EN 62366:2008.

concepts.name: Usability Validation Plan
concepts.evidence: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of ADD.

concepts.name: Usability metrics
concepts.evidence: Usability metrics refers to user performance measured against specific performance goals necessary to satisfy usability requirements.

concepts.name: Critical errors
concepts.evidence: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

concepts.name: Non-critical errors
concepts.evidence: Non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

concepts.name: Completion rate
concepts.evidence: Completion rate is the percentage of test participants who successfully complete the task without critical errors.

concepts.name: Subjective evaluations
concepts.evidence: Subjective opinions about specific tasks, time to perform each task, features, and functionality will be surveyed and collected via questionnaires.

concepts.name: Usability Engineering
concepts.evidence: EN 62366:2008 Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The document is titled Usability Validation Plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Plan for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on APR/18/2020.

evidence.claim: The document describes a test plan for usability testing during ADD development.
evidence.evidenceText: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of ADD.

evidence.claim: The usability test objectives include benchmarks, design problem identification, product design and production efficiency improvement, and end-user satisfaction maximization.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability test objectives are: To construct the benchmarks of user attributes To identify potential design problems to be solve To improve product design and production efficiency To complete end-user satisfaction maximization

evidence.claim: The usability test requires a total of 7 participants meeting the stated user profile.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants should be at least equal to the following User Profile used in the Usability test.

evidence.claim: The ADD will be used in a typical operating environment described in the usability engineering report.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD will be used in a typical operating environment described in usability engineering report.

evidence.claim: Usability metrics include scenario completion, dialog script adherence, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
evidence.evidenceText: Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, and subjective evaluations will be used. Time-to-completion of scenarios will also be collected.

evidence.claim: Critical errors are unresolved task errors or errors that produce an incorrect safety-related outcome.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

evidence.claim: Non-critical errors do not affect final task output but reduce task efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: Non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

evidence.claim: Primary operating usability scenarios include power, cable, detector operation, patient arrangement, and software calibration tasks.
evidence.evidenceText: Connecting the power cord to the power supply Connecting the Data Cable to the Power Supply and the Detector Connecting the LAN cable to the power supply Trigger cable connection to power supply LAN cable and PC main body connection Connecting the communication cable to the power supply Power on the detector Arrange the patient in the correct position to have detector aligned with the target body part Calibration of detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: Reasonably foreseeable worst-case scenarios include detector disconnection, lack of attention to detector readiness before X-ray exposure, wrong wiring, dropping, excessive force, wrong calibration, high temperature, water or humidity, patient positioning, reading and manipulation, mode selection, and network configuration.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the reasonably foreseeable worst case Identification Sequence of Events / Tasks as Performed by a Specified User During Normal Use Wiring disconnection to Detector => no reading Not attention to Detector’s ready before X-ray exposures => no reading or inappropriate image result Use Errors Wrong wiring of interface cable from X-ray machine to Detector Dropping Applying excessive force Wrong calibration in the control software Environment High temperature => failure of function Water/humidity => failure of electronic/electric shock Patient Positioning by holding a Detector Reading and manipulation Selection of mode before taking x-ray images Set up network configuration for use

evidence.claim: The usability completion goal is 100% for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: The usability error goal is total errors below 4 for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total errors of below 4 is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: A usability verification and evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.
evidence.evidenceText: The Usability verification & evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.

evidence.claim: The document references EN ISO 14971:2007 for medical device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2007 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The document references EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 for medical electrical equipment usability requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Usability

evidence.claim: The document references EN 62366:2008 for usability engineering for medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366:2008 Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text; authoritySignal is only 'general'.
Product model identifiers beyond ADD and Abyz Digital Detector are present in metadata/path signals but not clearly stated in the document body.
Some extracted wording appears to contain grammar or OCR/preprocessing artifacts, such as 'Are over 100% of operators' and duplicated section numbering.
